message:
frankenterm: wire IPC server through Cx-aware bind and run variants

Threads a Cx through the watcher's IPC server setup so the unix-socket
IPC path is structured/cancel-correct instead of starting from an
ambient runtime context.

- run_watcher() now resolves a Cx up-front for the IPC lifetime:
  Cx::current() when one is available, otherwise cx::for_request().
  That Cx is passed to IpcServer::bind_with_permissions_with_cx and a
  cloned copy is handed to the spawned task that calls
  run_with_registry_auth_rpc_and_search_config_with_cx.
- Behavior is unchanged on the happy path — the _with_cx methods are
  Cx-aware wrappers over the existing ones — but shutdown and
  cancellation now propagate through the IPC task the same way the
  rest of the watcher's asupersync-native tasks do.

Matches the broader ft-xbnl0 direction of removing ambient-runtime
assumptions from shipped code paths and making every long-running
task explicitly owned by a Cx.
